summary: Execute the GitHub repo update after the skill repo split. This public handoff contains all non-secret project context the server Codex needs. After reading this JSON, the server Codex must wait for one follow-up secret message containing the Hermes deploy key private key material, then continue.
project_rules: OpenClaw skill-body work belongs in the OpenClaw skill repo. At observation time the current GitHub name is community-skill, but the intended final GitHub name is community-skill-for-openclaw.
Hermes skill-body work belongs in community-skill-for-Hermes.
openclaw-for-community is still only a fresh-instance or template repo, not the skill-body release repo.
myproject must not be used as the place to store skill-body release work.
Do not assume repo names from old memory only. Verify actual remotes before pushing.
execution_requirements: First verify whether the user has already renamed community-skill to community-skill-for-openclaw on GitHub.
Update local git remotes if the OpenClaw repo rename has already landed.
Use the Hermes repo community-skill-for-Hermes for Hermes-side pushes.
Do not push Hermes changes into the OpenClaw repo.
Do not push skill-body work into openclaw-for-community.
This thread is for GitHub repository update only, not for self-hosted acceptance testing.

secret_followup_required.why: Hermes-side push may require a write-enabled deploy key that must not be stored in the public Logs repo.
what_server_codex_should_do: Then wait for the user's next message containing the secret local-only key block.
Install the key only after that follow-up arrives.
Do not claim blocked before waiting for the secret follow-up.

after_secret_arrives: Install the Hermes deploy key to the indicated server path.
Verify SSH access to the Hermes repo.
Push Hermes changes only to btmisgod/community-skill-for-Hermes.
Push OpenClaw changes only to the real OpenClaw skill repo name that exists at execution time.
success_contract: Return exact repo URLs, branch names, commit SHAs, and changed-file lists.
State explicitly whether the OpenClaw repo rename was already complete when you executed.
State explicitly whether the Hermes deploy key was installed and used.
State explicitly which repo received OpenClaw changes and which repo received Hermes changes.
